project_overview.project_development_objective: To increase investment, Small and Medium Enterprise (SME) growth, and entrepreneurship in non-mining productive sectors in the Recipient's territory.
project_overview.beneficiaries: Growth-oriented SMEs, entrepreneurs, destination communities, organizations, financial institutions, investors, line agencies, and entrepreneurship institutions. Particular focus on women-owned and led businesses and people with disabilities.

primary_locations: National coverage for business environment reforms
geographic_scope.coverage_type: national with regional focus on Western Peninsula and offshore islands for tourism
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: partial - Sierra Leone retains elements of fragility post-civil war (1991-2002). Country is not on fragile states list but has fragility characteristics. Project addresses fragility through economic diversification and institutional strengthening.

key_components.component_name: Component 1: Improving Business Environment and Capacity Building
key_components.description: Streamline government-to-business interactions through digitalization of business registration, construction permits, and land records. Strengthen access to finance infrastructure via Collateral Registry. Build institutional capacity of public sector through training and technical assistance.

Public goods investments in 6 tourism destination upgrades and 2 SME Solution Centers. Support entrepreneurship ecosystem through grants to institutions. Provide technical assistance and subgrants to start-ups and SMEs. Leverage private investment through co-investment mechanisms.
Project coordination, monitoring and evaluation, communications, citizen engagement, and grievance redress mechanism implementation.
Zero-budget component for disaster recovery contingency that may be triggered for eligible natural or human-induced crises. Can be used for COVID-19 response.

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Tourism & Cultural Affairs (MTCA), Ministry of Finance (MoF), Ministry of Trade and Industry (MTI), with Project Coordination Unit (PCU) within MoF. Project Fiduciary Management Unit (PFMU) handles all fiduciary functions.
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Project Steering Committee (PSC) chaired by Minister of Finance with representation from relevant ministries, agencies, and private sector. Technical Committee (TC) with TIMA focal points. Technical Working Groups around each component. Public-Private Dialogue consultations.
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers (July 2016, revised November 2017 and August 2018). Open competitive approach preferred. National procurement procedures may be used below defined thresholds. Project Procurement Strategy for Development (PPSD) prepared. Procurement management risk rated High, mitigated to Substantial.
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: PCU M&E Specialist responsible for results monitoring. Biannual progress reports to World Bank. Annual public event to report results. M&E framework with PDO and intermediate results indicators. Data collection through project records, surveys, and secondary sources. Gender-disaggregated data collection. Potential impact evaluation of SME support activities.

identified_risks.risk_description: Fragile context with post-election violence in March 2018. Main opposition APC has parliamentary majority, potential for policy disagreements. However, democratic consolidation improving.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Strong stakeholder ownership through Project Preparation Committee. Regular engagement with public and private sector stakeholders. Project Steering Committee with broad representation.

identified_risks.risk_description: High risk of persistent fiscal deficits, deterioration in terms of trade, lower FDI inflows, and financial sector weaknesses. COVID-19 pandemic exacerbates these risks. Projected 2020 economic contraction of 3.1 percent.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Government commitment to fiscal discipline demonstrated through IMF ECF program. Project support to SMEs and economic diversification helps build resilience. Contingent Emergency Response Component available for crisis response.

identified_risks.risk_description: Policies affecting private sector often implemented with limited private sector consultation. Weak public-private dialogue and low technical capacity of line ministries.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project provides technical support to beneficiary agencies and ministries. Promotes public-private dialogue through working groups and consultations for business environment, SME growth, and tourism development.

identified_risks.risk_description: Low institutional capacity increases implementation risk. Novel elements include SME Solution Centers and early-stage financing that may face underutilization or implementation challenges.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project design informed by analytical work on business constraints. Feasibility studies for SME Solution Centers to assess demand and government service availability. Early-stage financing approached as pilot, conditional on positive viability assessments.

identified_risks.risk_category: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability
identified_risks.risk_description: Substantial likelihood of weak institutional capacity. MTCA and MTI lack recent World Bank experience and have weak capacity. COVID-19 may divert attention of key personnel.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: PCU established within MoF with qualified personnel and strong project management experience. Operation manuals, oversight, and control mechanisms. Frontloading of capacity building. Technical specialists support TIMAs.

identified_risks.risk_description: Overall weak fiduciary environment. Limited evidence of value for money, economy, efficiency, effectiveness, integrity, transparency, and accountability. Weak data controls in FM systems. High fiduciary risk rating.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Government established PFMU with independent, qualified personnel. Internal control systems meeting COSO standards. New Government demonstrating commitment to accountability and anti-corruption. Development partners supporting FM weaknesses. Increased implementation support planned.

identified_risks.risk_description: Moderate risk. Potential environmental impacts from civil works (waste management, health and safety, erosion, water quality). Social risks include land acquisition, GBV in tourism areas (identified as hotspots for sex work and trafficking), and impacts on vulnerable groups.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: ESMF and RPF prepared and disclosed. Environmental and Social Management Plan with OHS plan, Labor Management Procedure, and GBV mitigation plan. Project recruits environmental and social safeguards specialists. GBV NGO recruited for mitigation and response. Universal design standards for accessibility. Grievance Redress Mechanism established.

identified_risks.risk_description: Multiple stakeholders across government, private sector, civil society, NGOs, communities, and development partners. Risk of information asymmetry and coordination failures.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project Preparation Committee established with cross-section of stakeholders. PSC and Technical Working Groups designed for coordination. Citizen engagement and regular dissemination of project information. Comprehensive mapping of private sector development programs by other partners to identify overlaps and collaboration opportunities.

identified_risks.risk_description: High risk from COVID-19 pandemic. Although country declared Ebola-free, COVID-19 presents major health threat that could shift government priorities to health services and humanitarian needs.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Existence of alert and surveillance system. Country's experience dealing with Ebola crisis. Support from World Bank and other organizations for COVID-19 response. Project includes CERC for emergency response. Project activities adapted to support COVID-19 response, particularly SME support.

safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: Yes - Project-level GRM housed in PCU at MoF, linked to all implementing agencies. Procedures for filing complaints and dispute resolution within acceptable timeframe. GBV-sensitive GRM to receive and manage project-related GBV complaints confidentially. Also accessible to World Bank's Grievance Redress Service (GRS) and Inspection Panel.
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Comprehensive gender focus: (1) Legal and regulatory reforms designed to promote women economic empowerment; (2) Collateral Registry improvements to benefit women entrepreneurs lacking immovable assets; (3) Specific pools and tailored support for women entrepreneurs with lower cash matching requirements; (4) SME Solution Centers with childcare support and gender-aware staff training; (5) Personal initiative training and GBV prevention programs; (6) Mentorship and internship programs for women entering male-dominated sectors; (7) Monitoring of women business registration, loans secured with movables, and follow-on investment. Gender analysis conducted. Women, Business and the Law (WBL) score of 63.1 (below SSA average of 69.9). Women face barriers in access to finance, technology, and self-assurance.

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project addresses conflict-related fragility through: (1) Economic diversification to reduce vulnerability to commodity price shocks that fueled conflict; (2) Job creation and income generation for youth and vulnerable populations; (3) Institutional strengthening and governance improvements; (4) Private sector development and investment attraction to build resilience; (5) Inclusive growth focus with emphasis on women and marginalized groups; (6) GBV prevention and response mechanisms in tourism areas identified as hotspots; (7) Community engagement and participatory planning in destination upgrades.
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Sierra Leone retains elements of fragility post-10-year civil war (1991-2002): (1) Weak institutions and poor governance undermine state legitimacy; (2) Limited structural economic transformation - economy dependent on mining (89% of exports) and agriculture; (3) High poverty (56.8%) and unemployment despite growth; (4) Low labor force participation (58.6%, below SSA average); (5) High gender-based violence (57% of women aged 15-49 experienced physical or sexual violence); (6) Vulnerability to external shocks (Ebola crisis 2014-2016 caused 21.5% economic contraction); (7) Climate change and natural disaster vulnerability (floods, landslides, coastal erosion); (8) Limited private sector development and entrepreneurship ecosystem; (9) Weak business environment (ranked 163/190 in Doing Business 2020); (10) Low access to finance (65.1% of firms cite as binding constraint).
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Project addresses security through: (1) Economic diversification reducing reliance on volatile mining sector; (2) Job creation and income generation reducing incentives for crime and instability; (3) Tourism sector development requiring safe, stable environment; (4) Infrastructure improvements in tourism areas enhancing security and safety; (5) GBV prevention design in tourism locations (proper lighting, open spaces, running water, separate toilets); (6) Code of Conduct requirements for contractors on Sexual Exploitation and Abuse; (7) Awareness raising on SEA and sexual harassment; (8) Grievance mechanisms for reporting security-related concerns.
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: No significant displacement anticipated. Project notes: (1) Physical displacements not anticipated for civil works; (2) Lands may be required for associated works which may obstruct business operation and loss of livelihoods in urban settings; (3) Resettlement Policy Framework (RPF) prepared to establish principles and procedures for follow-up Resettlement Action Plans (RAPs); (4) No physical/economic displacement will occur until RAPs finalized, approved by World Bank, and implemented; (5) Abbreviated RAPs may be prepared depending on scale of impact; (6) Community destination plans include citizen engagement and consultation; (7) Project includes provisions for compensation and rehabilitation if needed.
